title: dryopteris affinis subsp. persica
intro: Dryopteris affinis subsp. persica, also known as the golden spleenwort, is a subspecies of a species of fern native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris affinis subsp. persica include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
